message:
br-bd-uzmsl add NFS-style file handles + OpenFs encode/open_by_handle

name_to_handle_at(2) and open_by_handle_at(2) are routed through plain
FUSE_LOOKUP at the kernel boundary — what FrankenFS owns is the
generation-cookie semantics that make a stale handle return ESTALE
once the inode it pointed at has been freed and reallocated.

New `ffs_core::file_handle` module:
- `FILE_HANDLE_LEN = 12`: u64 inode + u32 generation, little-endian.
- `encode(ino, generation) -> [u8; 12]`.
- `decode(&[u8]) -> Result<(InodeNumber, u32), HandleError>` rejects
  buffers of wrong length with `EINVAL` semantics.
- `verify(ino, handle_gen, live_gen)` returns `HandleError::Stale`
  with full mismatch context on disagreement.
- `HandleError -> FfsError::Io(EINVAL|ESTALE)` conversion plus a
  `to_errno()` accessor so callers can translate to the libc errno
  that open_by_handle_at userspace expects.

`OpenFs` consumers:
- `encode_file_handle(ino)` looks up the inode, narrows the
  generation back to u32, and returns the 12-byte handle.
- `open_by_handle(&[u8])` decodes, runs `getattr` to fetch the live
  inode, and surfaces ESTALE via verify() before returning the attrs.

Tests: 655 ffs-core --lib pass (was 644 + 11 new). 7 unit tests on
the helpers cover encode/decode/verify happy + error paths and the
HandleError -> FfsError -> errno mapping. 4 OpenFs integration tests
cover encode-then-open round-trip, forged-generation ESTALE, wrong
length EINVAL, and unknown-inode lookup-error propagation. fmt +
clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_uzmsl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
